itemGroups.title: Core Build
itemGroups.insight: <ScItemInline itemName="Boots of Swiftness" /> helps you catch up to players without needing to land <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" />\n\nCan go <ScItemInline itemName="Mercury's Treads" /> or <ScItemInline itemName="Plated Steelcaps" /> if really needed though.

You have enough damage, now you just need to catch enemy carries. Default to <ScItemInline itemName="Dead Man's Plate" /> so you can run players down. Can go <ScItemInline itemName="Force of Nature" /> vs lots of magic damage.
itemGroups.title: Fourth Item Options

itemGroups.insight: Items that scale off max HP are OP for <ScChampionInline championId="Chogath" /> at this point. <ScItemInline itemName="Unending Despair" /> for armor. <ScItemInline itemName="Kaenic Rookern" /> for MR. If you need mixed resistances, can go <ScItemInline itemName="Jak'Sho, The Protean" />
itemGroups.title: Fifth Item Options
Sixth Item Options
itemGroups.insight: Default to <ScItemInline itemName="Jak'Sho, The Protean" /> 6th if you haven't bought yet. <ScItemInline itemName="Randuin's Omen" /> if crit ADC is a problem. <ScItemInline itemName="Unending Despair" /> is great vs short-ranged enemy comps.

sorcery-secondary-insight-03.insight:
With AP <ScChampionInline championId="Chogath" />, a lot of players run <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Axiom Arcanist" /> and <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Celerity" /> in their secondary tree instead, but this is **not** as effective as it might initially sound.
* With <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Ultimate Hunter" />, you already have quite a bit of ==Ult Haste==. And after you get ==6 stacks== of your ult, you're **not** spamming it on CD anymore, so you ==lose some value== on the rune.
* Your ult still deals more damage, but you also deal more damage with <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Cash Back" />'s ==free gold== anyway.
* This set up is a bit better early game to get ==6/6 free ult stacks faster==, but ==falls off== compared to the other. You can run whichever style you prefer.

approach-velocity-insight-02.insight: The best rune to fix <ScChampionInline championId="Chogath" />'s biggest weakness, his ==mobility==.\n* Lets you very quickly catch up to players you randomly <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" /> so you can actually ==combo them in time== before they can get away.\n* Also lets you follow up off your ==teammate's CC==, in case you miss your own <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" />. You just need to get into ==melee range==, and your kit will do the rest. This rune makes it much easier to get there.

hob-insight-01.insight: There's 2 styles to play <ScChampionInline championId="Chogath" />. There's the standard tank version, or the style where you go AP at first so you can reliably ==one-shot== squishy targets. If you want to hard carry and make life miserable for enemy carries, this is the build for you. \n* <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Hail of Blades" /> lets you proc your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" possessive /> ==3 hit buff== instantly, giving you ==absurd burst== in fights.\n* With some AP items, if you land a single <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" />, you can walk up, ==silence==, ==auto 3 times immediately==, and snack on a squishy target. It's extremely OP once you get going in a game.

runesv3.summonerSpellExplanation: Can go <ScSummonerInline summonerIdentifier="Ignite" /> vs healing tops to cheese kill them.

content.content: Both <ScItemInline itemName="Kaenic Rookern" /> and <ScItemInline itemName="Unending Despair" /> scale off your max HP, which <ScChampionInline championId="Chogath" /> gets plenty of.\n\nThese are your best MR and Armor items to build in the later stages once you've got a ton of HP.

can be a bit of a training dummy where auto-attackers get to hit you for free in fights. If they have lifesteal, you're kind of a walking health fountain sometimes.

If they have champions like and other drain-tankers, then go Otherwise will let you reach the enemy before she kills you.

insights.title: Double Value Every Trade
insights.reason:
You can start **Q** instead in hard match-ups and play for last-hits, it has nearly 1,000 range on a 6 second cool-down, making it super safe to farm and scale up. 

Otherwise start **E**, and mentioned before, <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Hail of Blades" /> lets you proc your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> extremely fast for easier trades. But there's ways to optimize it for higher value:

* Trading around ==minion waves== is often better for Cho than others. Your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> and <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="W" />'s ==AOE damage== will often kill minions in trades, giving you a ==push advantage==, or ==healing== from your passive.
* Or if you end the trade short after ==1-2== <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> autos, then you can try and finish the trade by <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" />'ing the minions, giving you the ==splash damage== on your opponent while walking away.

The threat of your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" /> is often ==far more potent== than the ability itself. Most players hyper-focus on dodging it, even if it's your last maxed ability. And if you miss, they feel way more confident in ==punishing you==.\n* Most top laners are either fast or have a ==mobility spell== in their kit\n* So **don't** throw random <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" />s. Always try to combo it with either <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" />'s ==slow== or <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="W" />'s ==silence== to make sure you land it consistently.
insights.title: Raw Q Is a Gamble
Win by Surviving
Stack Fast, Don't Hesitate
insights.reason:
As Cho, the real skill lies in optimizing stacking your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="R" />. There's no easy answer here. If you think you can score a kill, you should save your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="R" /> to fight. 

* Otherwise, just use it on a ==minion immediately==. **Don't** **hesitate.** The faster you use it, the faster it comes up.
* You can track your progress by holding down ==shift== and hovering your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="R" />. It'll tell you ==X/6== for how many stacks you've gotten from minions, so you know how many more =="free stacks"== you can get.
* At ==8 minutes==, it's really good to try and get priority over the wave just one time. Cho is REALLY good at walking over to ==Grubs== and killing one fast with his ult, getting a ==stack on his Ult==, and the objective of course.
